sourceMovie.title: Werewolves
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Werewolves (2024) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Werewolves clicked for you, Extinction (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Werewolves?
faq.answer: Try Extinction, The Fury, The Car—each shares DNA with Werewolves while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Werewolves?
faq.answer: Werewolves (2024) is best described as Action, Horror, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Werewolves worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
